title: Bashing the series for whiny emo characters
display_title: Bashing the series for whiny emo characters
wikitext:
[]: Bashing the series for whiny emo characters             Quote Post by VRChanticleer » Thu Mar 27, 2014 9:15 am I wrote an essay about this topic but unfortunately it got lost in a site glitch when I tried to post it. Whatever content is in it I'll just post it later as a response to posts in this thread. I'll just sum up my essay. NGE today gets bashed for having such a cast of whiny emo characters. Not just Shinji but the cast as whole . As a complete noob to the series who just finished weeks ago, I don't have an opinion as of now and am seeking long time fan's take on this criticism.

[]: Re: Bashing the series for whiny emo characters             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 27, 2014 10:01 am VRChanticleer wrote: I wrote an essay about this topic but unfortunately it got lost in a site glitch when I tried to post it. Whatever content is in it I'll just post it later as a response to posts in this thread. I'll just sum up my essay. NGE today gets bashed for having such a cast of whiny emo characters. Not just Shinji but the cast as whole . As a complete noob to the series who just finished weeks ago, I don't have an opinion as of now and am seeking long time fan's take on this criticism.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Thu Mar 27, 2014 10:09 am Also to just chime in here Asuka for instance witnessed her own mothers suicide (saw her dead body just hanging there with a rope tied around her neck) Show me a person that doesn't have a little emotional problems after that. Her acting the way that she acts is not emo She's a scared, lonely girl that wants to be loved and held.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Mar 27, 2014 10:29 am Yeah, that's something that many people don't take into account : most characters are damaged, but that's because most of them witnessed utterly horrible things on their life : - Shinji and Asuka saw their mother's death firsthand, which the added trauma for Shinji to be abandoned by his father a few days later without explanations. - Misato saw the bringer of the apocalypse and said apocalypse from a front-row seat, and the sacrifice of her father - Ritsuko saw her mother commit suicide after killing a child - if Kaji's backstory from the manga is also canon in the main series, he got an utterly shitty childhood having to fight and survive through the post-2I anarchy and saw his little brother and all of his friends die because he was forced to rat on them. And to add to that, all the adults had to live through the chaos just after 2I wars, which was all but stated to be a really shitty period, can you imagine just how much the world needs to plunge into chaos to have Tokyo suffer a nuclear bombing , and that kind of attack becoming the standard of conflict all around the world? Is it any wonder that everyone is messed in the head? NGE has a gallery of characters who have very real personal issues, some of them typical of teenagers, but exacerbated to the extreme by their past experiences and the surrounding circumstances, namely a world barely beginning to recover from a near apocalypse, and having to fight physic defying giant monsters wanting to finish said apocalypse.

[]: Re: Bashing the series for whiny emo characters             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Mar 27, 2014 10:37 am VRChanticleer wrote: NGE today gets bashed for having such a cast of whiny emo characters.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 27, 2014 1:14 pm Everybody gets pissed because IMAO they're expecting a protagonist that can do no wrong. Having a character that has flaws (gasp) is obviously unfit to take on the role of MC.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Mar 27, 2014 1:19 pm No. There is difference between characters with flaws and character forced into not his genere just to be out of place for the sake of drama.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Mar 27, 2014 3:34 pm Come on Stillborn... EoE asside, Shinji DID have the makings of a hero. It's the fact that we've seen him 'man up' in the past and punch the impossible square in the nuts that makes his whole boneless sack of meat routine in that movie so infuriating. As to such whiny/emo issues, I think anyone dismissing the charecters in this fashion should stick to Michael Bay movies and leave the rest of us alone. We'll all be happier that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Mar 27, 2014 3:39 pm I mean I have another dark series as comparison in Berserk. Guts is flawed character. His biggest fuck up was chasing after his vengeful obsession while leaving Casca alone whe she needed support the most. And he still is enjoyable character. The problem with Shinji is that he is portrayed pretty much as "the only incompetent amongst competent" that makes his character so hard to bear. And even if he as you said does punch impossible in the face, he is immidietly knocked down from whatever backbone he gained.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Mar 27, 2014 3:57 pm Incompetent? He's far and away the most effective pilot when it comes to actual combat conditions even after you dismiss the times Yui bails him out. Rei does what she's told but generally doesn't have the drive to actually win on her own because she wants to die. Asuka has a vaneer of professionalism but as soon as the plan runs into difficulty she falls to pieces. By contrast, the more the pressure is on the less Shinji's issues get in his way, allowing him to vent all his pent-up emotions at a target he's allowed and encouraged to destroy. He's dangerously unstable and a loose cannon, but as long as he's pointed at the enemy he's the best weapon in Nerv's arsenal, bar none. Outside of the eva I'll give you your interpretation, but shouldn't the new guy who didn't get any training or preparation be the fish out of water? It would be ridiculous if he wasn't.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:04 pm Fish out of the water is reasonable at the beginning but not all the time. And Q took it to another level entirely by putting Shinji not only on square one but in the negative values field. After 3/4 of the plot has passed.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:14 pm ^ Then why do you bother defending him or lamenting how bad his situation is, when it's clear that you view Shinji as both useless in the grand scheme and incompetent? You chastise Anno for creating this supposed "unfair" world, yet you criticize and debase Shinji because he isn't the character equipped for this world. Which is it dude?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:14 pm He remains a fish out of water because nobody ever bothers to tell him anything that is actually going on until the very end. He bounces from one crisis to the next and is only told what he needs to hear for the issue immediately at hand, so of course he never gets a chance to find his stride. At least some of this, however, is necessary for the story to work; doing otherwise would kill the mysteries that drive the plot beyond 'angel of the week' levels. I'll agree with you regarding his treatment in Q, but in contrast to EoE it actually has me squarely in his corner.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:21 pm ^^ I'm not criticizing him. I'm lamenting over role that has been pinned to him and which he cannot lose, no mater how he squirm because universe will beat him into his designed path. Both meta and in universe.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:32 pm ^ So you're just lamenting on how Shinji is supposed to take on this "role" in his story as an Eva pilot, and that your trying to say it would be better if Shinji never had this "role".....But then Evangelion wouldn't exist without that conflict! Shinji is one of the main reasons Evangelion IS Evangelion, because it's his interpersonal struggles with this role that make it so unique. He wouldn't have had such an impact to audiences that hit so close to home, because he's so realistic and flawed.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:32 pm Stillborn wrote: ^^ I'm not criticizing him. I'm lamenting over role that has been pinned to him and which he cannot lose, no mater how he squirm because universe will beat him into his designed path. Both meta and in universe.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:41 pm Why thank you. If you didn't told me that I wouldn't know you don't like me. Why I'm here? I don't know. Maybe I'm trying to understande where this "greatness" of Eva that everyone and their dog is talking about. Or what's so brillian in Anno being troll. And maybe other reasons, like being able to talk with someone here, without being accused of of being to shallow for "T3H GREATEST SHOW".

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:51 pm Stillborn wrote: Why thank you. If you didn't told me that I wouldn't know you don't like me. Why I'm here? I don't know. Maybe I'm trying to understande where this "greatness" of Eva that everyone and their dog is talking about. Or what's so brillian in Anno being troll. And maybe other reasons, like being able to talk with someone here, without being accused of of being to shallow for "T3H GREATEST SHOW".

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Thu Mar 27, 2014 4:51 pm I'm still totally at a lost on how Shinji is 'emo' His mother died and his dad left him all the a very young age. He has trouble relating to others and unable to find happiness in life. How does that make him emo?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 27, 2014 5:00 pm Stillborn wrote: Why I'm here? I don't know. Maybe I'm trying to understande where this "greatness" of Eva that everyone and their dog is talking about.
